heading: Time of payment of premium charges
label: 1735f–8 Time of payment of premium charges
text: In carrying out the provisions of subchapters I, II, IV, VII, VIII, IX–B, and X pertaining to the payment of loan or mortgage insurance premium charges by a financial institution, other mortgagees, or agent thereof to the Federal Government in connection with a loan or mortgage insurance program established pursuant to any of these subchapters, the Secretary shall require that payment of such premiums be made (1) in the case of loans or mortgages respecting one- to four-family residences, promptly upon their receipt from the borrower, and (2) in any other case, promptly when due to the Secretary; except that the Secretary may approve payment of such premiums within twenty-four months of such receipt or due date, as appropriate, if the financial institution, mortgagee, or agent thereof pays interest, at a rate specified by the Secretary, to the insurance fund for the period beginning twenty days after receipt from the borrower or after the due date, as appropriate, and ending upon payment of the premiums to the Federal Government.1See References in Text note below.1
notes: References in TextSubchapter IV of this chapter, referred to in text, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 101–73, title IV, § 407103 Stat. 363Aug. 9, 1989

Amendments1989— struck out reference to subchapter IX–A after reference to subchapter VIII.Pub. L. 101–2351983— substituted “(1) in the case of loans or mortgages respecting one- to four-family residences, promptly upon their receipt from the borrower, and (2) in any other case, promptly when due to the Secretary” for “promptly upon their receipt from the borrower”, inserted “or due date, as appropriate,” after “such receipt”, and inserted “or after the due date, as appropriate,” before “and ending”.Pub. L. 98–181
